agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA and premium fee. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders. Multiple contradictions exist between stakeholder positions that must be resolved before implementation.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of the Order Fulfillment context, intersecting with Driver Management, Billing/Refunds, and Customer Tracking.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a time-bound delivery window (target: 2 hours) and an additional fee, subject to eligibility constraints."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A $12.99 surcharge applied to orders that select the Express Delivery option, on top of the base delivery fee."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge applied to all orders regardless of express selection. Not refundable under the express SLA."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The time-bound target (2 hours from order placement) within which an express delivery should be completed."},{"term":"Express Eligibility","definition":"The set of preconditions a customer must meet to select Express Delivery (e.g., proximity to warehouse)."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The internal operational target of 15 minutes from order placement for a driver to pick up an express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Qualified Driver","definition":"A driver eligible to fulfill express orders, currently defined as having a rating of 4.0 stars or above."},{"term":"Missed Window","definition":"The event where an express delivery is not completed within the Express Window, potentially triggering a refund."},{"term":"Driver Cancellation","definition":"The event where an assigned driver cancels an express delivery mid-fulfillment, requiring reassignment."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute target to assign a replacement driver after a driver cancellation. Failure may trigger an automatic refund."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A refund of the Express Fee issued when the Express Window is missed. Per Legal, this is not a contractual obligation but a discretionary business policy."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, which disqualifies them from receiving a Goodwill Refund."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express Delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a physical constraint — if the customer is beyond 10km, the 2-hour window is operationally impossible. Allowing it would corrupt SLA tracking data and create guaranteed refund situations.","testable_assertion":"The system must reject Express Delivery selection when the calculated distance between the delivery address and the nearest warehouse exceeds 10km."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by Finance or Product leadership.","testable_assertion":"When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total includes an additional $12.99 line item labeled as the Express Fee."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute pickup target is an operational policy tuned to make the 2-hour window achievable. Operations could adjust this threshold.","testable_assertion":"If no driver has picked up an express order within 15 minutes of placement, the system flags the order as at-risk and triggers an escalation."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible to be assigned express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Driver rating thresholds are operational policy, adjustable based on driver supply and demand.","testable_assertion":"The system must exclude drivers with a rating below 4.0 from the express order assignment pool."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When an Express Window is missed, the Express Fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Upon a Missed Window event, the system creates a refund record for exactly the Express Fee amount ($12.99) and does not refund the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong delivery address, even if the Express Window is missed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a refund eligibility policy that could be revised by Finance or Customer Success.","testable_assertion":"When a Missed Window event occurs and the order is flagged as Wrong Address Exception, no refund is created."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Express Fee refunds are processed within 3 business days of the Missed Window event.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is an operational SLA, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"All approved express refund records must have a processing completion date no later than 3 business days after creation."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-minute replacement window is an operational target, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a Driver Cancellation event occurs on an express order, the system initiates reassignment and tracks elapsed time. If 10 minutes elapse without reassignment, an automatic refund is triggered."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers can track their express driver's location in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Real-time tracking is a product feature, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"While an express order is in transit, the customer-facing interface displays the driver's current GPS location with updates at intervals no greater than 30 seconds."},{"id":"BR-10","rule":"The Express Window commitment must be displayed prominently to the customer during order placement and tracking.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"UI display requirements are product decisions.","testable_assertion":"The express delivery time target is visible on the order confirmation screen and the order tracking screen."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse (boundary condition)","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — Must define whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommendation: inclusive (<=10km qualifies)."},{"scenario":"Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars while carrying an active express order","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — The rating threshold should apply at assignment time only. An in-progress delivery should not be revoked due to a rating change mid-delivery."},{"scenario":"No express-qualified drivers are available within 15 minutes","expected_behavior":"Order becomes at-risk. System should escalate but still attempt delivery. If window is ultimately missed, standard Goodwill Refund policy applies."},{"scenario":"Customer provides a wrong address but corrects it within minutes — delivery still misses the window","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — Does the Wrong Address Exception still apply if the customer corrected the address? Need policy on partial-fault scenarios."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 110 of a 120-minute window — 10 minutes for replacement would exceed the window","expected_behavior":"Automatic refund should trigger because the window will inevitably be missed regardless of reassignment success."},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same express order","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — Does each cancellation restart the 10-minute replacement clock? At what point is the order considered failed?"},{"scenario":"Warehouse goes offline or runs out of stock after express order is placed","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — No stakeholder addressed supply-side failures. Refund policy and customer communication for this case are undefined."},{"scenario":"Customer places express order at 11:55 PM — 3 business day refund processing spans a weekend","expected_behavior":"3 business days excludes weekends and holidays. A Friday missed window would process by Wednesday."},{"scenario":"Customer's GPS location is within 10km but the actual road distance exceeds 10km","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — Must define whether the 10km eligibility check uses straight-line distance or road/driving distance. Operationally, road distance is what matters for delivery time."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"New revenue stream at $12.99 per express order. Refund liability exposure on missed windows — must be modeled against expected miss rate. Wrong-address exception reduces refund exposure. Automatic refund on failed reassignment creates an uncapped liability if driver supply is thin.","operational":"Requires real-time driver tracking infrastructure, sub-15-minute dispatch capability, driver rating integration into assignment logic, and automated refund processing pipeline. The 10-minute reassignment SLA demands a robust driver pool or escalation to non-express drivers.","user":"Customers get a premium fast-delivery option with transparency (real-time tracking, prominent SLA display). The refund policy builds trust but the Legal vs PM contradiction on 'guarantee' language risks customer confusion and potential complaints if expectations are set too high.","risk":"HIGH — The guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the single biggest risk. Marketing 'guaranteed 2-hour delivery' while Legal says it is not a guarantee creates legal exposure, customer trust erosion, and potential regulatory issues in some jurisdictions. Additionally, automatic refunds triggered by the 10-minute reassignment failure could be exploited or could cascade during driver shortages.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"New premium option with faster delivery. Risk of confusion over guarantee vs best-effort language. Refund policy improves trust but wrong-address exception may frustrate some customers."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"New order category with eligibility requirements (4-star minimum). Express orders create time pressure. Driver cancellation triggers visible consequences (replacement clock, potential auto-refund)."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Must build or adapt dispatch to enforce 15-minute pickup, 10-minute reassignment, distance-based eligibility, and rating-based driver filtering. Real-time monitoring required."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"New fee revenue, new refund liability line. Must build automated refund processing within 3-business-day SLA. Need forecasting for refund rates."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"New escalation paths for missed windows, driver cancellations, and wrong-address disputes. Need training and tooling for express-specific cases."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Must resolve guarantee language before launch. Goodwill refund framing must be reflected in all customer-facing copy, terms of service, and marketing materials."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Real-time tracking, distance calculation, automated refund triggers, driver rating integration, and SLA monitoring all require significant implementation effort."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Confidence is low because the spec contains a fundamental unresolved contradiction (guarantee vs best-effort) that affects the entire product's customer-facing contract, refund policy framing, and legal exposure. Additionally, several operational scenarios lack defined behavior (stock-outs, multiple cancellations, distance calculation method, partial-fault address corrections). Acceptance criteria cannot be finalized until the contradictions are resolved.","high_confidence_areas":["Express Fee amount ($12.99) is agreed upon by all parties","10km eligibility constraint is clear and uncontested","15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver rating are clear operational rules","Wrong-address exception to refund is clear","Base delivery fee is never refunded under express SLA"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort — this changes everything downstream","Whether automatic refund on failed reassignment is truly automatic or requires approval","How 'wrong address' is determined and disputed","Distance calculation method (straight-line vs road distance)","Behavior under supply-side failures (no drivers, no stock)"],"assumptions":["The 10km distance check is performed against the nearest warehouse, not a specific warehouse","Driver rating is a rolling average, not a per-delivery score","The 2-hour Express Window starts at order placement, not at pickup","Express orders follow the same base delivery flow with additional constraints layered on top","Only one express fee refund is issued per order regardless of how many issues occur"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' and Customer Success wants it 'shown prominently' as a guarantee. Legal explicitly states this CANNOT be a guarantee — it is a 'best effort' SLA and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This is a fundamental contradiction that affects all customer-facing language, marketing, terms of service, refund policy framing, and potential legal liability. The product cannot launch with both positions.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Marketing","Customers"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — frame as 'Express target' with goodwill refund. Reduces legal risk but weakens marketing appeal.","B: Negotiate with Legal for a qualified guarantee — e.g., 'guaranteed or your express fee back' (making the refund the remedy, not damages). This may satisfy both sides.","C: Adopt PM's position and accept legal risk — not recommended."],"recommendation":"Option B — a qualified guarantee where the express fee refund IS the contractual remedy. This gives customers a clear promise while capping legal exposure at $12.99 per order. Legal must review and approve the exact language."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success says refund should be 'automatic' when replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These timelines conflict — is the refund decision instant (auto-triggered) with processing taking up to 3 days, or does the 3-day window include the decision? Additionally, Finance's wrong-address exception may conflict with Customer Success's automatic refund expectation.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Finance","Customers"],"options":["A: Auto-approve the refund immediately upon trigger, but process the actual payment within 3 business days.","B: Auto-flag for review, with a 3-business-day processing window that includes approval."],"recommendation":"Option A — the refund decision is automatic and immediate upon trigger (missed window or failed reassignment), but the financial processing (actual money movement) occurs within 3 business days. The wrong-address exception should be checked before auto-approval."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No stakeholder defined what constitutes a 'wrong address.' Is it a non-existent address? A typo the driver can resolve? An address in a different city? Who determines fault — the driver, an algorithm, or a CS agent? This directly affects refund eligibility and will be a major source of customer disputes.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Finance","Customers"],"options":["A: Define wrong address as one that fails geocoding validation at order time — this shifts responsibility to the system.","B: Define wrong address as one where the driver reports inability to deliver and CS confirms — this is manual and slow.","C: Combine both — validate at order time AND allow driver to flag, with CS as final arbiter."],"recommendation":"Option C — validate addresses at order placement to catch obvious errors (reducing disputes), but allow drivers to flag wrong addresses with photo evidence, and CS makes final determination for refund eligibility."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No stakeholder addressed: (1) Express availability hours — is it 24/7 or business hours only? (2) Maximum order size/weight for express. (3) Geographic rollout scope. (4) What happens if demand exceeds driver supply — is there a cap on concurrent express orders? (5) How distance is calculated (straight-line vs road). (6) Whether express can be combined with other promotions or discounts.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Operations","Engineering","Customers"],"options":["Conduct a follow-up workshop to resolve these gaps before finalizing the domain model."],"recommendation":"Schedule a focused follow-up session with PM and Operations to define express availability hours, capacity limits, distance calculation method, and geographic scope. These are blocking questions for implementation."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot define acceptance criteria due to unresolved guarantee vs best-effort contradiction","business_rule_broken":"The fundamental customer contract for Express Delivery is undefined — is it a guarantee with a contractual refund remedy, or a best-effort target with a discretionary goodwill gesture? Every acceptance criterion depends on this answer."},{"violation":"Automatic refund semantics are contradictory between Customer Success and Finance","business_rule_broken":"BR-5, BR-7, and BR-8 cannot be coherently specified until the team agrees on whether 'automatic' means 'instantly decided, processed later' or 'flagged for review within 3 days.'"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA and premium fee. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders. Multiple contradictions exist between stakeholder positions that must be resolved before implementation.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of the Order Fulfillment context, intersecting with Driver Management, Billin
